extraction.one_line_summary: GALL Felix won from breakaway at Courchevel after O'Connor teammate setup, while VINGEGAARD destroyed POGACAR by 5m47s on final climb to take 7m35s GC lead
tactical_patterns: O'Connor sacrificed breakaway position to launch teammate Gall on final 200m ramp, teammate setup decisive on steep finish
Pogacar crashed early touching wheels, same side as pre-race wrist injury, signaled vulnerability before final climb collapse
Vingegaard gained 5m47s on Pogacar in final kilometers alone, suggesting GC decided by sustained power not punchy attack
Yates finished 2nd despite being GC rider, suggesting UAE/Jumbo allowed breakaway rather than controlling for sprinters

rider_signals.POGACAR Tadej: negative: early crash same side as broken wrist then lost 5m47s in final km suggests accumulated fatigue breaking physically
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: positive: gained time in manner of great champions per commentary, 7m35s lead eliminates seconds-based tactics for remaining stages
rider_signals.GALL Felix: positive: converted Swiss Tour form three weeks prior directly to TDF stage win, indicating peak timing capability
rider_signals.O'CONNOR Ben: positive: willingly sacrificed own result to launch Gall on final 200m, reveals Jayco prioritizes stage wins over individual placings
rider_signals.YATES Simon: positive: finished 2nd as GC rider suggesting he read breakaway would succeed and infiltrated rather than defending
rider_signals.BILBAO Pello: positive: outsprinted Vingegaard for 3rd showing Bahrain form peak, UAE isolation of Pogacar evident
rider_signals.POWLESS Nels: negative: dropped from early break and straight through peloton on first climb, extreme fatigue after KOM jersey effort
